query: Pixie: Your AI agent that works, researches, and gets things done alternative

results.title: 12 AI Agents That Actually Do Your Work in 2026 (Not Just Chat About It)
results.content:
### If you need an agent to own entire projects

Go with Manus. Hand it a research task, content project, or data analysis — it plans, executes, verifies, and delivers. Best for discrete, well-scoped projects.

### If you want a proactive assistant that drafts in your inbox

Go with Lindy. It sorts your inbox, drafts replies in your voice, and texts you from iMessage — as long as you’re fine reviewing and sending the drafts yourself. If you’d rather the work get finished, Carly is the more reliable alternative. For a broader comparison, see best AI agent platforms.

### If you need to automate websites

Go with ChatGPT Agent for anything that requires interacting with actual web UIs. Go with Bardeen for recording and replaying browser-based workflows on a budget. [...] Pricing: 7-day free trial. Paid plans from $49.99/month.

Best for: Individuals who want a proactive assistant in their inbox and iMessage and are happy reviewing and sending its drafts. For more options in this category, see best AI agent platforms.

Limitations: Lindy drafts and waits for your approval, so you’re still the last step on every action, and it can stall on multi-step work. If you’d rather the work just get done, Carly is the more reliable alternative — its agents have their own email address and finish the job end-to-end.

### 4. ChatGPT Agent (Operator) — Best for Browser Automation

What it is: ChatGPT Agent is OpenAI’s autonomous browsing agent that interacts with websites, fills forms, makes purchases, and completes web-based tasks on your behalf. [...] Limitations: Google ecosystem only. Enterprise pricing locks out individuals and small teams. Still maturing. For cross-platform needs, consider a Google CC alternative that works across ecosystems.

### 9. AgentGPT — Best for DIY Agent Building

What it is: AgentGPT is an open-source platform for creating autonomous AI agents. Define a goal, and AgentGPT breaks it into subtasks, executes them, and iterates until completion.

How it works: Give it an objective (“research competitors in the CRM space and create a comparison report”). It builds an execution plan, spawns sub-agents for each task, gathers information, and delivers a result. Self-host or use the cloud version.

results.title: My AI Agent Team — 7 AI Tools For Getting Things Done | by Andrew Baisden | Medium
results.content:
It’s also still one of the best coding LLMs out right now, especially when it comes to producing clean and readable code. If I’m focusing on an article, a tech review or a deep work session, Claude is a good choice.

## 3. Qwen The Researcher

Qwen is one of the up-and-coming stars in my AI rotation. It’s a great tool for data collection, analysis and research. It can hunt down citations, detail studies and sort through niche technical topics pretty well.

It’s also good at coding, maybe not as slick as ChatGPT or Claude, but fast and flexible. I use Qwen when I want an alternative or want to see a different perspective on something.

ChatGPT and Claude often take the lead; Qwen is a great alternative for some tasks or when the others are busy.

## 4. Grok The Real-Time News Expert [...] And because all of these models are at or near the top in LLM leaderboards, I know that it’s giving me some of the best reasoning, knowledge and insight out there at the moment. Below is the full lineup of my team of AI agents, how I utilise them, what they excel at and why you might want to build some yourself.

## 1. ChatGPT The All-Around Powerhouse

ChatGPT is my all-purpose assistant. It’s excellent for the coders, thinkers and content writers, planning daily activities or creating images. Whether I’m writing code or brainstorming ideas, ChatGPT gets the job done. [...] It’s not always as wild and inventive as ChatGPT or Claude, but it’s solidly reliable, and of course, so deep in Google’s ecosystem, which makes it ideal for when I’m researching notes in Google Docs or Drive, or even browsing YouTube videos.

I use Gemini all the time when I’m looking for a second opinion on something technical or factual. I guess you could say it’s my “grounded” agent, which is less opinionated and more balanced. Image creation is right up there with the best platforms, too.

## 6. DeepSeek The Document Analyst

DeepSeek is one of my favourite tools for when you need something more like an ask-heavy research task. It’s pretty impressive because you can upload and then analyse large numbers of files.

That’s where Simular Pro stands out: an autonomous, transparent computer agent that can live inside your workflows the way a real teammate would—on your desktop, in your browser, and across your cloud stack. Start by giving it one painful workflow—say, weekly campaign reporting or prospect research—and let it earn its keep.

results.title: 12 Best AI Agents in 2026: My Top Picks for Automation, Research, and Daily Work
results.content:
## Lindy: Best AI Agent for Personal Work Assistance

Lindy stands out as one of the most accessible AI agent platforms available today. Instead of drag-and-drop builders or complex configuration screens, you simply describe what you want your agent to do in plain English — and it gets to work.

Whether it's triaging your inbox, updating your CRM, or reaching out to leads, Lindy turns those instructions into reliable automated workflows without requiring a single line of code. For busy teams in sales, HR, or customer support, that kind of simplicity is genuinely refreshing. [...] 7min
